title: Erumbeeswarar Temple
tagline: Erumbeeswarar Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Erumbeeswarar Temple in Thiruverumbur, Tamil Nadu, India, is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva.
overview.description: Erumbeeswarar Temple in Thiruverumbur, Tamil Nadu, India, is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva. Built on a 60-foot (18 m) tall hill, it is accessible via a flight of steps. The temple's main shrines and its two prakarams (outer courtyards) are on top of the hill, while a hall and the temple tank are located at the foothills. Shiva is believed to have transformed himself into an ant hill and tilted his head at this place to enable ants to climb up and worship him.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Erumbeeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Erumbeeswarar Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Erumbeeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (7 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (9 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Erumbeeswarar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Erumbeeswarar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Erumbeeswarar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Erumbeeswarar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Erumbeeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thiruverumbur taluk, Ellakudy, Nehru Stadium, Tiruchirappalli, Pappankurichi, Koothappar.
